params.text:
The hierarchy: Avoid → Assess → Reduce

MHOR sets out a clear order of priorities for tackling handling risk. Always work through it in this order:

  • Avoid hazardous manual handling wherever it is reasonably practicable — can the task be done a different way, or does the person even need to be moved right now?
  • Assess any hazardous handling that cannot be avoided, using a suitable and sufficient risk assessment.
  • Reduce the risk of injury so far as is reasonably practicable — for example by using equipment, changing the environment or sharing the task.

The goal is never simply to "lift more carefully". It is to remove or shrink the risk before anyone lifts at all.

params.alt: A mobility hoist used for safe transfers

params.taskDescription: Put the three steps of the manual handling hierarchy in the correct order, and complete the sentence.
params.textField: First, *avoid* hazardous handling wherever you can. If it cannot be avoided, carry out a risk *assessment*. Finally, *reduce* the remaining risk so far as is reasonably *practicable*.

params.question: If a handling task looks risky, the first thing to consider is whether it can be avoided altogether.

l10n.wrongAnswerMessage: Actually it is true. 'Avoid' comes first: before assessing or reducing risk, check whether the hazardous task can be avoided altogether.
l10n.correctAnswerMessage: Correct. 'Avoid' sits at the top of the hierarchy — always ask first whether the risky task is necessary or can be done another way.
